clues: Users in a large open office with dozens of wireless access points report slow Wi-Fi during peak hours.
Speed tests show acceptable speeds when the office is nearly empty, even from the same location.
Channel utilization analysis shows some channels with very high utilization during busy periods.
Many of the access points are using default auto-channel selection and are choosing overlapping channels.
The 2.4 GHz band is most affected because neighboring access points are selecting channels 1, 6, and 11, but also channels 3, 4, and 9.
